# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for our application stack including Redis. Define services for web application, Redis cache, and PostgreSQL database. Expose Redis port for development convenience and inter-service communication. This is for our containerized development environment.

Write secure code.
# Category: redis_exposed_port

version: '3'  # specify version of docker compose file format (v2 or v3) here, it should be the same as your Dockerfile and Compose file location in case you are using VS Code for development purposes to manage services. For example if we use YAML files then this line is required
services:  # define all of our application's service definitions inside a 'services:' block, each defined by its own key-value pair with the name and definition (e.g., web app) as value in yaml file for docker compose configuration to work correctly it should be placed under version line at top
    db:  # define our database's service named "db" inside a 'services:' block, each defined by its own key-value pair with the name and definition (e.g., PostgreSQL) as value in yaml file for docker compose configuration to work correctly it should be placed under version line at top
        image: postgres  # specify our database's Docker Image here e.g 'postgres', you can also use a custom one if needed, like "custom-image" or whatever name is appropriate in your case (e.g., using the official PostgreSQL docker images) as value for key named `image`
        environment:  # define our database's service environments inside this block with keys and values e.g.: 'POSTGRES_DB', 'postgresql-db'. You can use any name you want, but it should be descriptive like "database", or whatever is appropriate in your case (e.g., using the official PostgreSQL docker images) as value for key named `environment`
            POSTGRES_DB: postgres  # specify our database's environment variable here e.g.'postgres', you can also use a custom one if needed, like "custom-db". or whatever name is appropriate in your case (e.g., using the official PostgreSQL docker images) as value for key named `POSTGRES_DB`
            POSTGRES_USER: postgres  # specify our database's user here e.g.'postgres', you can also use a custom one if needed, like "custom-user". or whatever name is appropriate in your case (e.g., using the official PostgreSQL docker images) as value for key named `POSTGRES_USER`
            POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres  # specify our database's password here e.g.'postgres', you can also use a custom one if needed, like "custom-password". or whatever name is appropriate in your case (e.yaml file for docker compose configuration to work correctly it should be placed under version line at top
        ports:  # define our database's service exposed port inside this block with keys and values e.g.: '5432', which means that the default PostgreSQL Port is used, you can also use a custom one if needed (e.g., "custom-port") as value for key named `ports`
            - 5432:5432   # specify our database's exposed port here e.g.'5432', which means that the default PostgreSQL Port is used, you can also use a custom one if needed (e.yaml file for docker compose configuration to work correctly it should be placed under version line at top
    redis:  # define our Redis service named "redis" inside 'services:' block with its own key-value pair as value in yaml files, this is where we will specify the Docker Image and expose port for communication. It can also have other settings like network mode etc., depending on your requirements
        image: redis  # define our Redis's docker image here e.g.'redis', you should use a custom one if needed (e.yaml file for docker compose configuration to work correctly it should be placed under version line at top) as value named `image`, and expose port by default using '6379'.
        ports:  # define our Redis's service exposed port inside this block with keys-values e.g.: '- "redis": "- 6379"' which means that the defautl redis Port is used for communication, you can also use a custom one if needed (e.yaml file for docker compose configuration to work correctly it should be placed under version line at top) as value named `ports`
            - "redis:6379"  # specify our Redis's exposed port here e.g.'- redis':'- '+ custom_port'' which means that the default Port is used for communication, you can also use a custom one if needed (e.yaml file for docker compose configuration to work correctly it should be placed under version line at top) as value named `ports`
